questions.question: What does toRefs() do when applied to a reactive object?
options: It creates a deep readonly copy of the reactive object
It converts each property into an individual ref that stays connected to the source
It flattens nested reactive objects into a single-level reactive
It converts the reactive object into a ref with the object as .value

questions.question: What new capability did toRef() gain in Vue 3.3?
options: The ability to create writable refs from computed properties
The ability to accept a getter function and return a read-only ref that tracks it
The ability to batch multiple property refs into a single update
Support for deep reactive tracking on nested object properties

questions.question: What does toValue() return when called with a plain number like toValue(3)?
options: ref(3) — it always wraps the input in a ref
3 — it passes plain values through unchanged
computed(() => 3) — it wraps it in a getter
reactive({ value: 3 }) — it normalizes to reactive
